title: Discontinuity-Free Decision Support with Quantitative Argumentation Debates
abstract: We introduce a novel formalism for decision support, Quantitative Argumentation Debate (QuAD) frameworks, which extends the existing formalism of Argumentation Debates by equipping arguments with an intrinsic strength. We propose a novel semantics for QuAD frameworks, based on a discontinuity-free scoring function that avoids the problems of existing approaches. We prove a number of desirable properties of this semantics, and describe a case study demonstrating the use of QuAD frameworks for decision support.
venue: Principles of Knowledge Representation and Reasoning (KR-16)
